name: cable one arm pulldown
instructions: Attach a single handle to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handle with an overhand grip and extend your arm fully.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the handle down towards your side while keeping your elbow close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your lat muscle.
Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other arm.

Attach a single handle to a high pulley cable machine. Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grasp the handle with an overhand grip and extend your arm fully. Keep your back straight and your core engaged. Pull the handle down towards your side while keeping your elbow close to your body. Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your lat muscle. Slowly release the handle back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other arm.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
